medications.indication_rationale: Relieves colicky smooth-muscle spasm in a functional colitis flare, provided surgical red flags are absent.
medications.dose_route_frequency: per standard OTC/label dosing as required (adult)

medications.indication_rationale: Opioids mask evolving surgical pathology and worsen ileus; inappropriate for an undifferentiated acute abdomen via telehealth.
A GP/gastroenterology maintenance decision, not an acute telehealth prescription tonight.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Apply gentle heat, lie on the left side with knees drawn up, and take a bland diet for 24 hours.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Eases spasm and rests the bowel while awaiting resolution.

patient_education_points.point: This is very likely a flare of your microscopic colitis triggered by the rich meal — painful bowel spasm and watery diarrhoea, not a blockage. Because you're still passing wind and stool, aren't vomiting, and your tummy isn't swollen, a blockage is unlikely.
Use an antispasmodic, apply gentle heat, and stick to bland foods; it usually eases over 12-24 hours.
Go to ED if the pain becomes constant and severe, your tummy becomes hard/swollen and you stop passing wind, you start vomiting, you develop a fever, or you pass blood.

follow_up_plan.interval: GP within 1-2 weeks (sooner if not settling)
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Symptom resolution and maintenance therapy (e.g. budesonide); in-person examination if pain persists.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Constant/severe or localised pain, obstipation with vomiting, fever, or bloody stool — ED.

must_include_items: Screen obstruction/appendicitis/perforation red flags before attributing to a flare
Offer antispasmodic + simple measures for a functional colitis flare
Strict surgical-abdomen safety-net and a low threshold for in-person review (abdomen not palpable remotely)
Recognise abundant stool/flatus argues against obstruction
acceptable_alternatives: Bland diet and heat as symptomatic care
errors_of_commission: Reflex midnight ED/CT for a resolving functional flare with no red flags
Prescribing opioids for an undifferentiated acute abdomen
Reassuring without any red-flag screen or in-person threshold
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing requires a surgical red-flag screen PLUS symptomatic management with a clear safety-net. Reflex CT/ED and unscreened reassurance both fail.
